mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k jobs/month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect increased construction activity and consumer confidence, which correlate with stronger equity market performance over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods where HOUST growth exceeds 2% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals slack in the labor market, prompting central bank intervention that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling interest rates (FEDFUNDS) create a 'stagflation' environment that depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 275 and FEDFUNDS is below 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of periods with consecutive FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing activity that supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods where INDPRO growth exceeds 1% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) reflects stronger economic growth that boosts corporate profits and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of quarters with GDP growth above 2%.
